title: The U.S. World Cup team’s biggest lineup debate has Mass. ties at its core
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Matt Freese and Matt Turner are contenders for the U.S. World Cup starting goalkeeper position.
Freese, a Harvard graduate, is favored by U.S. coach Mauricio Pochettino for his technical skills.
Matt Turner has excelled in advanced statistics, including 'goals prevented', challenging Freese's starting role.

title: New England Aquarium to celebrate World Ocean Day with free waterfront events on Sunday
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The New England Aquarium celebrates World Ocean Day with free events at Central Wharf from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m.
Activities include a life-sized inflatable whale, indigenous fishing demonstrations, and an interactive climate art mural.
Free Ocean Dreams screenings at Simons Theatre; shuttles from Dorchester and Roxbury require pre-registration.

title: Review & setlist: 5 Seconds of Summer keep the fans at the center of an unforgettable show
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 5 Seconds of Summer performed for over 12,000 fans at TD Garden during their "Everyone's A Star!" tour on June 5, 2026.
The band entertained with a theatrical structure of six acts featuring humor, crowd participation, and fan-voted surprises.
The setlist included new songs like "NOT OK" and classics such as "Amnesia", culminating in an encore with "Youngblood".

title: What 17th-century advice seekers had in common with us
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Boston Public Library event on June 30th explores centuries of relationship advice with Meredith Goldstein and historian Mary Beth Norton.
Norton's book examines love advice from the 17th century showcasing issues like in-law troubles shared across generations.
The event features discussions on various topics, including the evolution of advice columns and personal relationships.

title: New on Netflix and more: Top streaming picks for June 2026
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix adds 'Creed', 'The Big Lebowski', and 'Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse' to its June 2026 lineup.
Disney+ streams 'Hoppers', a new Pixar film where a teen transforms into a beaver to save her grandmother's glade.
Apple TV releases 'Cape Fear' series starring Amy Adams and Patrick Wilson, with Javier Bardem as the antagonist.

title: Ex-Yankee Sonny Gray earns first victory against New York as member of Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray earned his first win against the Yankees as a Red Sox player, pitching 6.1 innings in a 5-3 victory.
Gray stated he disliked playing for the Yankees during his 2017-18 stint, influencing his move to the Red Sox.
Gray has a 7-1 record with a 3.20 ERA this season, contributing to a strong Red Sox rotation.

title: Willson Contreras rides highs and lows of Red Sox win over Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a 2-run homer for the Red Sox against the Yankees in a Friday night game.
Contreras expressed frustration after a collision in the eighth inning, spiking a Gatorade cooler in the dugout.
Contreras leads the team with 13 home runs and is batting .299, second behind teammate Mickey Gasper.

title: Severe thunderstorms could hit Mass. Saturday evening, forecasters say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Severe thunderstorms with hail and damaging winds could hit Massachusetts Saturday afternoon and evening, per NWS forecasts.
Communities west and northwest of Interstate 95 have the highest risk of severe weather, including potential storm warnings.
Boston area could see severe weather between 4-8 p.m., with threats of localized flooding and hail damage possible.

title: Nantasket Beach stayed quiet despite fears of a social media-fueled ‘takeover’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hull police increased staffing at Nantasket Beach over fears of a social media-organized event but reported no incidents.
Authorities initially prepared for a 'takeover' event following similar incidents at other New England beaches in previous weeks.
Boston's Carson Beach was rumored as a potential new location for gatherings, with no reports of incidents or arrests.

title: Red Sox’ Payton Tolle talks ‘playing with joy’ for self, team: ‘I try to come with a smile every day’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Payton Tolle, a Boston Red Sox pitcher, made his MLB debut against the Pittsburgh Pirates in August 2025.
Tolle often expresses strong emotions on the field, using his excitement strategically to boost team momentum.
Dedicated to his late mother, Tolle aims to 'play with joy', showcasing infectious energy and a positive attitude.
